orientation.description: Canvas orientation. 'vertical' → 1080×1920 (9:16, default), 'horizontal' → 1920×1080 (16:9). Resolves width/height and picks each frame's layout.

capHighlight.description: Karaoke spoken-word highlight color (hex). Defaults to #FFD24A; independent of each frame's accent so captions read as one system.

description.description: YouTube description (2-4 sentences). Written to meta.json; not used at render.

hashtags.description: YouTube hashtags (3-6, lowercase, no #). Written to meta.json; not used at render.

tags.description: YouTube search-keyword tags (5-10 phrases). Written to meta.json; not used at render.

audio.description: Single full-script narration path relative to videos/<slug>/. Written by the alignment step; replaces the old per-scene scenes[].audio.

voiceStyle.description: Metadata for LLM pipeline; not used at render

id.description: Unique kebab-case id → filename and composition-id

type.description: Frame type selector. Every type renders in both orientations at the canvas size resolved from config.orientation (vertical 1080×1920 by default, horizontal 1920×1080).

typeUsage.cover: DEFAULT for the opening scene / topic intro. A safe general choice.
typeUsage.stats: use when the scene centers on ONE striking number or figure.
typeUsage.diagram: DEFAULT for general explanatory content — a set of labelled ideas/steps with no dedicated visual. A safe general choice when nothing more specific fits.
typeUsage.cta: DEFAULT for the closing scene / takeaway / call to action.
typeUsage.atom: use ONLY when the narration describes a specific element's atomic structure or electron shells.
typeUsage.molecule: use ONLY for a specific named molecule/compound whose atoms are being described (e.g. NaCl, H₂O).
typeUsage.ph-bar: use ONLY when the narration is specifically about acidity, alkalinity, or the pH scale.
typeUsage.orbital-overlap: use ONLY when the narration is specifically about orbitals overlapping/sharing between two atoms.
typeUsage.bond-comparison: use ONLY when directly contrasting an ionic bond vs a covalent bond.
typeUsage.particle-count: use ONLY when the narration counts/quantifies particles or ions (e.g. concentration).
typeUsage.tug-of-war: use ONLY when the narration frames two atoms competing/pulling for electrons.
typeUsage.element-card: use ONLY when the narration is specifically about a single element's properties (atomic number, mass, symbol, etc.)
typeUsage.reaction-equation: use ONLY when the narration is specifically about a chemical reaction and its reactants/products. Show the reaction equation with labels.
typeUsage.quote: use ONLY when the narration is a pull quote or citation. Show the quote text and attribution.

start.description: Computed by scripts/align-captions.mjs from real narration timestamps once that pipeline has run; hand-authored otherwise.

duration.description: Computed by scripts/align-captions.mjs from real narration timestamps once that pipeline has run; hand-authored otherwise.

captions.description: Naturally-split caption chunks displayed sequentially during the scene. Also doubles as the scene's expected narration text (chunks joined with spaces) for scripts/align-captions.mjs.

captionTiming.description: Populate-computed, not hand-authored. Written by the alignment step from real Whisper word timestamps: one {text,start,end,words} entry per captions[] chunk, start/end in scene-local seconds. words[] carries per-token {text,start,end} for the karaoke highlight. If absent, populate.js falls back to evenly spacing captions[] across duration and synthesizes words[].

words.description: Per-word timing for the karaoke highlight; one entry per whitespace token, scene-local seconds.

items.description: Sticker chip labels (diagram). Each entry is either a plain string label, or { label, color } for a per-chip background color (e.g. pH markers: 0 red, 7 white, 14 blue) — do not mix in unsupported shapes like nested objects.

statLabel.description: Short label under the stat, e.g. 'more acidic' (stats)

subheadline.description: Secondary line under the headline, one short sentence (cta)

symbol.description: Short chemical element symbol only, 1-3 characters, e.g. 'H', 'O', 'Na'— NEVER a descriptive phrase like 'Sodium atom'. Rendered large and prominent (a small circle badge on atom, a big card heading on element-card) — will look wrong or overflow if it's not a real short symbol. (atom, element-card)

title.description: Scene title label — meaning differs by type: for atom, the element's full name + symbol, e.g. 'Sodium — Na'; for molecule, the compound name/formula, e.g. 'NaCl' or 'H₂'; for ph-bar, a short heading label, e.g. 'THE pH SCALE'. (atom, molecule, ph-bar)

r2.description: Second reactant, optional — omit if the reaction only has one reactant (reaction-equation)

p2.description: Second product, optional — omit if the reaction only has one product (reaction-equation)

leftSymbol.description: Short chemical element symbol only, 1-3 characters, e.g. 'Na' — NEVER a descriptive phrase. Renders inside a small fixed-size circle and will visually overflow if too long. (molecule, tug-of-war)

centerSymbol.description: Short chemical element symbol only, 1-3 characters. Optional — only set for a 3-atom molecule with a center atom (e.g. H2O's O); omit for a simple 2-atom pair. (molecule)

rightSymbol.description: Short chemical element symbol only, 1-3 characters, e.g. 'Cl' — NEVER a descriptive phrase. Renders inside a small fixed-size circle and will visually overflow if too long. (molecule, tug-of-war)

centerColor.description: Center atom color. Optional, pairs with centerSymbol. (molecule)

angle.description: Bond angle in degrees. 180 = atoms side-by-side (typical for a diatomic pair like H₂ or NaCl); other values angle the bond, mainly useful with a center atom. (molecule)

atom1.description: Short chemical element symbol only, 1-3 characters, e.g. 'H' — NEVER a descriptive phrase like 'Atom A'. Renders inside a small fixed-size circle and will visually overflow if too long. (orbital-overlap)

atom2.description: Short chemical element symbol only, 1-3 characters, e.g. 'H' — NEVER a descriptive phrase like 'Atom B'. Renders inside a small fixed-size circle and will visually overflow if too long. (orbital-overlap)

ionicAtom1.description: Short chemical element symbol only, 1-3 characters — the electron donor, e.g. 'Na'. Renders inside a small fixed-size circle and will visually overflow if too long. (bond-comparison)

ionicAtom2.description: Short chemical element symbol only, 1-3 characters — the electron acceptor, e.g. 'Cl'. Renders inside a small fixed-size circle and will visually overflow if too long. (bond-comparison)

covalentAtom1.description: Short chemical element symbol only, 1-3 characters, e.g. 'H'. Renders inside a small fixed-size circle and will visually overflow if too long. (bond-comparison)

covalentAtom2.description: Short chemical element symbol only, 1-3 characters, e.g. 'H'. Renders inside a small fixed-size circle and will visually overflow if too long. (bond-comparison)

particleLabel.description: Short label shown on each particle icon, e.g. 'H⁺' — 1-3 characters, renders inside a small glyph. (particle-count)

targetCount.description: Number of particle icons to count up to, typically 5-20 for visual clarity (particle-count)

containerLabel.description: Label for the container/vessel holding the particles, e.g. 'Strong acid' or 'pH 2 solution' (particle-count)

result.description: Short concluding label shown once the count-up completes, e.g. 'Highly acidic' (particle-count)

mode.description: 'equal' = both atoms pull with the same strength (covalent-leaning, they end up sharing); 'unequal' = one atom dominates (ionic-leaning, it ends up winning outright) (tug-of-war)

electrons.description: Number of electrons being contested in the tug-of-war, typically 1-2 (tug-of-war)

verb.description: Present-tense verb describing the pull/interaction shown, e.g. 'pulling', 'winning' (mode: unequal), 'sharing' (mode: equal) (tug-of-war)
